text.title: Multi-Tenancy Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Multi-Tenancy Patterns` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Isolation model MUST be chosen against the strictest compliance scope (HIPAA / PCI / SOC 2 / GDPR-special-category). Down-isolation requires legal signoff.
rule.rationale: Choosing isolation by cost first creates incidents when a regulated customer signs.

rule.statement: ADR MUST state the blast radius of a single-tenant compromise as a number — tenants exposed, rows exposed, cost-to-recover.
ADR MUST document the migration path to the next stricter isolation tier (e.g. schema-per-tenant → DB-per-tenant) before launch.
rule.rationale: No-migration-path = re-architecture under fire when compliance changes.

rule.statement: Shared-everything model MUST declare per-tenant rate limits + resource quotas. Unbounded = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Unbounded shared tier = first-bad-tenant takes the rest down.

rule.statement: Every row in shared tables carries `tenant_id` with NOT NULL + index; per-tenant scoping enforced in the data layer (RLS or middleware).
rule.rationale: Missing tenant_id at row level = cross-tenant data leak under bug.

rule.statement: Per-tenant backup / restore path MUST be tested per quarter, not just per-region.
rule.rationale: One-tenant restore from a shared backup is impossible without per-tenant snapshots.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
